summary: Iteration 3 frontend-only verification (auth UX + trust polish). All 13 review items PASS via live Next.js preview + source review. Public routes /signin /explore /about /privacy /terms return 200; protected routes return 307 -> /?auth=required. Landing has dual hero CTAs (hero-cta-join + hero-cta-signin), explore link (hero-cta-explore), trust signals row (Google-authenticated profiles · Mobile-first · Free to join), Why-section with 3 GapCards + bridge sentence, expanded 4-column footer with About/Privacy/Terms read-more links and mailto:valueweave.team@gmail.com (data-testid=footer-contact). Navbar trio (nav-explore/nav-signin/nav-join) present and mobile responsive at 375px (Explore + Why anchor hidden, Sign in + Join visible, no horizontal overflow). No '2,400+' or '2400+' fake-growth strings present anywhere on landing. /signin page renders Welcome back heading, signin-loading spinner briefly, signin-google button, and 'Create your profile →' link to /get-started. /explore renders explore-title, search, category filter chips, opportunity list (live data present), bottom Join/Sign in CTA card, and AppNavbar in anon state (nav-public-signin/nav-public-join). /get-started shows 'STEP 1 OF 3 · NEW MEMBERS' badge and getstarted-signin-link -> /signin. /about /privacy /terms use shared LegalShell with h1 + back-home link. btn-primary in globals.css uses shadow-md shadow-amber-500/20 (intensity reduced as requested).
verified_checks: Landing: hero-cta-join, hero-cta-signin, hero-cta-explore, nav-explore, nav-signin, nav-join, footer-contact all visible; class on hero-cta-join is 'btn-primary' (which globals.css resolves to shadow-md /20).
Trust signals row: 'Google-authenticated profiles', 'Mobile-first', 'Free to join' all rendered with Shield/Smartphone/Sparkles icons.
Why section anchor #why renders cards: 'Talent without visibility', 'Ideas without teams', 'Trust without proof' plus the bridge sentence containing 'missing bridge'.
Footer: 4 columns (logo blurb + About + Privacy + Terms); read-more links to /about /privacy /terms all present; contact href = mailto:valueweave.team@gmail.com.
No '2,400+' / '2400+' substring anywhere in landing HTML.
Mobile 375px: nav-explore hidden, Why anchor hidden, nav-signin & nav-join visible; scrollWidth==clientWidth==375 (no horizontal overflow).

/signin: signin-loading appears initially then resolves to ready state showing 'Welcome back' heading + signin-google button + 'New to ValueWeave? Create your profile →' link with href=/get-started. Source code lines 14-29 correctly redirect already-authed users to /dashboard or /onboarding based on profile.profile_complete.

/explore: explore-title/search/explore-filter-all all rendered; live opportunity list shows real entries (Farm to Door Rice, 2D and 3D Designer using Revit, Tution Centre); bottom Join CTA card has explore-join + explore-signin testids; AppNavbar resolves to anon state (nav-public-signin/nav-public-join/nav-public-explore visible after auth check settles).
/get-started: chip text 'STEP 1 OF 3 · NEW MEMBERS' present; getstarted-signin-link href=/signin.

/about /privacy /terms: shared LegalShell (about/page.js line 22, re-exported & imported by privacy & terms); each renders h1 ('About ValueWeave','Privacy Policy','Terms of Service') and a back-home link <a href='/'>; visually consistent.
globals.css line 24: btn-primary = '@apply ... shadow-md shadow-amber-500/20 ...' (down from shadow-lg /30); line 30: btn-teal same pattern. btn-secondary has no shadow (outline style) — consistent.

/connections empty state copy (source verification, page is auth-gated): line 69 conditional renders 'No requests yet' for tab=received vs 'You haven\'t sent any requests' for tab=sent; line 77 CTA label conditional 'Post an opportunity →' for received vs 'Browse feed →' for sent.
critical_code_review_comments: Minor: privacy/page.js and terms/page.js import { LegalShell } from '../about/page'. While this works in Next.js (because LegalShell is a named export from a 'use server' module by default), it couples three route files via a sibling route module. Cleaner pattern is to extract LegalShell into components/LegalShell.jsx so the about route doesn't double as a barrel file. Not a bug — purely organisational.

AppNavbar uses authed === false strict-equality gating for the anon CTA cluster, so during the very first render (authed=null) those CTAs are hidden for ~100-300ms while supabase.auth.getUser() resolves. This is a deliberate flicker-avoidance choice and works correctly, but if you want instant first-paint for unauthenticated visitors consider rendering a skeleton/placeholder CTA cluster while authed===null.

/signin useEffect dependency includes [supabase, router] but supabase is created via createClient() on every render. Since the effect short-circuits after a successful redirect this is fine in practice; tightening to an empty dep array (run once on mount) would be slightly more deterministic.
Page imports across routes look clean: no hardcoded URLs, all auth uses window.location.origin/auth/callback (correctly), metadataBase only set in layout.js.
test_credentials: Not required. Per request, live Google sign-in completion not attempted on the preview because the preview redirect URL is not whitelisted in Supabase.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 3 fully verified. All public routes return 200, all protected routes redirect properly, all data-testids match the spec, mobile responsive at 375px is clean, button shadow polish landed in globals.css. The only optional follow-ups are non-blocking: (1) move LegalShell out of app/about/page.js into its own components file to avoid route-file-as-barrel pattern; (2) if first-paint flicker on AppNavbar anon CTAs becomes a concern, add a static placeholder while authed===null. /connections improved empty-state copy is verified by source only — to validate live, a seeded test session is required.
